title: manihot caerulescens subsp. paraensis
intro: Manihot caerulescens subsp. paraensis, also known as Para cassava, is a plant species within the Euphorbiaceae family. It is a type of cassava plant that is native to the tropical regions of South America. The Para cassava plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system.
